question_text: Let 𝑋1, 𝑋2, 𝑋3, 𝑋4 be a random sample of size 4 from a population having uniform

options.D: istribution over the interval (0, 𝜃), where 𝜃> 0 is an unknown parameter. Let 𝑋(4) = max{𝑋1, 𝑋2, 𝑋3, 𝑋4}. To test 𝐻0: 𝜃= 1 against 𝐻1: 𝜃= 0.1,
options.C: onsider the critical region that rejects 𝐻0 if 𝑋(4) < 0.3. Let 𝑝 be the probability of the Type-I error. Then 100⁡𝑝 equals ___________ (rounded off to two decimal places).
